sections.title: A: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each of the numbered words or phrases in bold is incorrect. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: Last month, I wrote to a few local television **station** asking if they gave away **the** free tickets to any shows.
A week later, they sent **to me** two tickets for a quiz show.
I went with my best friend Angie last night. It was **an** excellent!
The show is called Know Them Or Not? and contestants have to answer questions about **a lots** of different people in their lives, like friends, relatives and colleagues.
There are ten contestants to start with but one of them gets knocked out at the end of each round.
I'd like to go into **on** a show like that one day but, for now, I'm **much** happy just to have been in the audience.

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: Why can't (POLITICS) ...... ever just say 'yes' or 'no' when they're asked if they agree with something?
I was hoping this book would help me with my history project but in fact it wasn't very (INFORM) .......
My cousin Dan has kept a (JOURNAL) ...... since he was twelve years old.
Did you hear that (ANNOUNCE) ...... on the radio about the water shortage?
There's an (WRITE) ...... rule in our house about how late we can stay up to watch TV.
I just stood there in (BELIEVE) ...... when I heard I'd been selected to go on the quiz show.
The Internet is an incredibly powerful means of (COMMUNICATE) .......
I wouldn't describe the film as a comedy, but one or two scenes are mildly (HUMOUR) .......

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
These two dictionaries are similar. **little**
There ...... these two dictionaries.

My first editor greatly influenced me. **influence**
My first editor ...... me.

They described the robber on the evening news. **gave**
They ...... the robber on the evening news.

Robert Maxdoch has just bought the newspaper. **control**
The newspaper is now ...... Robert Maxdoch.

You'll be wasting your time if you try to get tickets for that show. **point**
There ...... to get tickets for that show.

I believe that adverts do influence people's buying habits. **view**
It ...... that adverts do influence people's buying habits.

The prime minister will probably make a statement this afternoon. **likely**
The prime minister ...... a statement this afternoon.

A programme about the royal family will be shown instead of Friends this evening. **place**
This evening, Friends, ...... a programme about the royal family will be shown.

The Hollywood star wouldn't say anything about the allegations. **comment**
The Hollywood star refused ...... the allegations.
How ...... information do we have at the moment?
Only a ...... print journalists manage to move into television.
This magazine's got ...... of adverts in it.
I won ...... money on a quiz show once.
There's ...... chance of getting rich from starting a website.
I think that ...... children's TV programmes these days are educational.
There aren't ...... opportunities for poets to get published.
The programme usually ...... on at half past eight.
I saw the interview while I was ...... through a magazine at the hairdresser's.
Why did you have to bring that subject ...... at dinner?
They mentioned it in the article but they didn't ...... into detail.
The tabloids completely ...... that story about Bruce Willis. It's not true at all!
They were ...... out free tickets to the concert to everyone in the shopping centre.
We had the volume turned down, so I couldn't make ...... what they were talking about.
